story_clusters.summary: Multiple High Courts have quashed or upheld GST-related actions based on procedural grounds. The Delhi HC set aside a bank account attachment after the petitioner furnished ₹1.5 crore security, while the Madras HC ruled that registration suspension without a proper show-cause notice (SCN) was unwarranted. Additionally, the Rajasthan HC upheld GST appeals, stating the 90-day limit under Section 101(2) is directory, and the HP HC invalidated an SCN relying on an unchallenged advance ruling.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expected to maintain its current policy rate at its upcoming Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, with multiple sources suggesting a pause on rate hikes amid concerns over inflation and growth. Economic indicators, including GDP data, will be closely monitored alongside global factors such as US trade talks. The decision aims to balance inflation risks while supporting economic recovery.

articles.title: Delay in Filing Return Condoned U/s 119(2)(b); ITAT Holds Section 80AC Bar Cannot Survive, Restores U/s 80P Deduction Claim
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a delay in filing tax returns, condoned by the competent authority under Section 119(2)(b), overrides the statutory bar imposed by Section 80AC. As a result, the assessee's claim for deduction under Section 80P must be reconsidered. The decision restores the taxpayer's eligibility for the deduction, setting a precedent for similar cases involving late-filed returns.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that condonation of delay by the competent authority removes the statutory obstacle created by Section 80AC for late-filed returns. As a result, the assessee's claim for deduction under Section 80P must be reconsidered in accordance with law. The post Delay in Filing Return Condoned U/s 119(2)(b); ITAT Holds Section 80AC Bar Cannot Survive, Restores U/s 80P Deduction Claim appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Section 148 Notice Was Issued After Limitation Expiry
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment for Assessment Year 2015-16 was invalid because the tax department issued a notice under Section 148 after the six-year limitation period had expired. The notice, issued on April 23, 2022, was time-barred as the deadline was March 31, 2022. Consequently, the reassessment proceedings and the resulting tax order were quashed. This ruling underscores strict adherence to procedural timelines in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that for AY 2015-16, the six-year limitation period under the unamended law expired on 31.03.2022. Since the notice under Section 148 was issued on 23.04.2022, it was time-barred. The reassessment proceedings and consequential assessment order were therefore quashed. The post ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Section 148 Notice Was Issued After Limitation Expiry appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a requirement for in-service teachers to qualify the Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) under the Right to Education (RTE) Act, stating that children's educational future cannot be compromised. The court extended the compliance deadline for this rule to August 31, 2028. The ruling pertains to the Anjuman Ishaat-e-Taleem Trust case and reinforces the child-centric nature of the RTE Act. The decision impacts teacher qualifications and compliance timelines across India.
articles.raw_summary: The RTE Act is a child centric legislation and must be read so. Service of teachers cannot come at the cost of educational future of the children. articles.title: SC Examines Constitutional Validity of Section 16(2)(c) as Bona Fide Recipient Denied ITC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is reviewing whether Section 16(2)(c) of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act is constitutionally valid. The provision denies input tax credit (ITC) to a recipient even if the tax was paid by their immediate supplier. The case questions whether a bona fide recipient should be penalized for alleged defaults by a supplier further up the supply chain.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court took up a challenge to Section 16(2)(c) of the CGST Act where ITC was denied despite tax having been paid by the immediate supplier. The case raises the issue of whether a recipient can be penalized for alleged default by a supplier further up the supply chain. articles.title: RBI launches the Survey on Foreign Liabilities and Assets of Mutual Funds: 2025-26 round
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank hereby launches the 2025-26 round of its annual survey on  Foreign Liabilities and Assets of Mutual Funds . The survey collects the information from mutual funds on their external financial liabilities and assets as at end-March of the financial year 2025-26. The aggregated survey results are disseminated in the public domain and are used in the compilation of India s external sector statistics. Mutual funds are required to fill the survey schedule, which is available on the RBI

articles.title: Being in electoral rolls no lifelong guarantee—what SC said on 1995 judgment at heart of SIR verdict
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission of India's (ECI) Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process, clarifying that being on electoral rolls does not guarantee lifelong inclusion. The court ruled that the presumption of validity in a 1995 judgment does not restrict the ECI's power to conduct SIR. The decision reinforces the ECI's authority to maintain accurate electoral rolls, ensuring fair elections in India.
articles.raw_summary: Upholding poll panel’s Special Intensive Revision, SC explains ‘context’ of 1995 decision. Presumption of validity is no ‘blanket embargo’ on ECI power to conduct SIR, court rules.

articles.title: MP High Court Flags 'Lack Of Coordination' Between Police, Prosecution; Says If IO Can't Be Served Summons, Status Of Other Witnesses Imaginable
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has flagged a 'total lack of coordination' between the police department and the prosecution after observing that, in a murder case the testimony of a Sub-Inspector could not be recorded because of failure to serve summons to him.� The bench of Justice Gajendra Singh opined, "If even a Sub-Inspector of the Police Department could not be served, then the status of the other witnesses can well be imagined. The prosecution appears to be completely unaware of the same.

articles.summary: The Jharkhand High Court ruled that a writ petition challenging a GST show cause notice (SCN) is maintainable because it raises a pure question of law regarding composite versus mixed supply. The court found a prima facie jurisdictional issue, allowing the petition to proceed. This decision impacts how GST disputes involving legal interpretations will be handled in the future.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the dispute involved a pure legal question regarding composite versus mixed supply. Finding a prima facie jurisdictional issue, it ruled that the writ petition was maintainable. articles.title: CSR Donations Eligible for Section 80G Relief as Section 37 Disallowance Does Not Bar Chapter VI-A Deduction
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that donations eligible for Section 80G tax relief under Chapter VI-A are not disqualified even if they are made as part of Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) obligations. The decision follows earlier rulings in the same case, clarifying that Section 37 disallowance does not bar such deductions. This impacts how companies claim tax benefits for CSR spending.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that donations qualifying under Section 80G do not become ineligible merely because they are incurred as part of CSR obligations. The deduction was allowed following earlier decisions in the assessee’s own case. The post CSR Donations Eligible for Section 80G Relief as Section 37 Disallowance Does Not Bar Chapter VI-A Deduction appeared first on TaxGuru .

The ruling is binding on tax authorities and taxpayers.

The chip includes a 20-core Grace CPU and Blackwell RTX GPU, offering up to 1 petaflop of AI performance and 128GB of memory. This allows users to automate tasks and interact using natural language without relying on cloud-based AI services.
articles.raw_summary: Nvidia's new RTX Spark chip will enable Windows laptops to run AI agents locally, allowing users to automate tasks and use natural language. The chip features a 20-core Grace CPU and Blackwell RTX GPU, offering up to 1 petaflop of AI performance and 128GB of memory.

articles.title: Conviction Can't Be Based On Evidence Recorded Prior To Summoning Accused U/S 319 CrPC: Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court recently held that evidence recorded in the absence of an accused person, which is relied upon to summon him under Section 319 CrPC, cannot later form the basis of his conviction. A bench of Justice Rajesh Singh Chauhan and Justice Subhash Vidyarthi observed thus while acquitting a murder accused in connection with a 2008 case. The trial court had convicted the appellant under Sections 302 r/w Section 149, Section 307 r/w Section 149, Section 148, and Section 506(2) IPC,

articles.title: Increase In Supreme Court Strength Through Ordinance-A Critique
articles.summary: It has become fashionable to talk of increasing the judge-strength to cope with the mounting work and pendency in courts. That is spoken of even about the highest court. Such talk is translated into action by increasing the number of judges. The Constitution originally fixed the strength of the Supreme Court to be eight-the Chief Justice of India and seven other judges. The Constitution enables Parliament by law to increase the strength. While PSU banks' structural tailwinds have largely played out, private banks are better positioned for sustained growth.

ITAT Deletes Black Money Act Penalty Due to Bona Fide Schedule FA Omission
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted a penalty imposed under the Black Money Act against a taxpayer who omitted to disclose foreign investments in Schedule FA. The ITAT found that the omission was bona fide, as the funds were sourced from disclosed banking channels and tax-paid income, with no deliberate concealment. The ruling highlights the importance of intent in tax penalty assessments under the Black Money Act.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT found that the taxpayer's foreign investment was funded through disclosed banking channels and tax-paid funds. Since there was no deliberate concealment, the penalty was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes Black Money Act Penalty Due to Bona Fide Schedule FA Omission appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CESTAT Quashes Service Tax Demand as Mining Services Were Not Taxable Before 1 June 2007
articles.summary: The CESTAT ruled that mining services like excavation and extraction of iron ore were not taxable under service tax before June 1, 2007. The tribunal held these activities were mining-related, not Business Auxiliary Services, and thus no service tax could be levied for the prior period. The decision impacts past tax demands and clarifies the taxability of mining services.
articles.raw_summary: CESTAT held that excavation, extraction, grading, and sorting of iron ore were mining-related activities and not Business Auxiliary Services. Since Mining Services became taxable only from 01.06.2007, no service tax could be levied for the prior period. The post CESTAT Quashes Service Tax Demand as Mining Services Were Not Taxable Before 1 June 2007 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Is It Permissible to Convert a Life Sentence into a Fixed Sentence? Supreme Court Clarifies
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified the conditions under which a life sentence can be converted into a fixed-term imprisonment, outlining the limits of judicial discretion. The ruling provides legal guidance on sentencing practices, impacting how courts handle such cases in the future. articles.title: Supreme Court Monthly Criminal Law Digest: April 2026
articles.summary: Anticipatory Bail - Routine filing and entertainment of anticipatory bail applications in private complaint cases is unnecessary, as mere issuance of summons/process does not entitle the police to arrest the accused. Sessions Courts and High Courts have no jurisdiction to direct an accused to surrender before the trial court while rejecting an anticipatory bail application in a complaint case. The Court may reject the application but cannot compel surrender. Om Prakash Chhawnika v. State of Jhar

This mechanism is part of the 'Beed formula' and impacts farmers and government finances amid concerns about a potential super El Nino affecting crops.

articles.summary: China has been blocked from importing advanced chip-making machines since 2019, limiting its ability to produce cutting-edge semiconductors. Services growth is expected to stay robust, but manufacturing may see slower expansion.

The strong participation, especially among women, highlights ongoing discussions about electoral engagement and gender parity in local governance.

RBI’s total foreign income rose 27% to ₹3.28 lakh crore, helped by higher returns from foreign securities, while domestic income increased 26% to ₹1 lakh crore due to higher interest on rupee securities.

articles.title: ‘Gaddi’ vs private property: The royal inheritance conundrum & how SC settled it with Kapurthala ruling
articles.summary: The Supreme Court settled a 50-year-old property dispute involving princely states like Dholpur, Rampur, and Kapurthala, clarifying the distinction between 'Gaddi' (royal inheritance) and private property. The ruling provides the clearest resolution yet on how royal properties should be inherited and managed. The case involved long-standing legal ambiguities over the rights of former royal families to their ancestral properties.
articles.raw_summary: From Dholpur   Rampur to Kapurthala, courts have grappled with matters of princely properties. Top court’s latest order in 50-year-old property dispute offers the clearest resolution yet.

The state argues that higher duties would support domestic farmers and stabilize prices.

articles.title: Predator-prey relationships and habitat quality shape a tiger reserve
articles.summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. After reintroduction, a study found that prey availability and habitat quality are crucial for tiger population recovery. The findings underscore the need for balanced conservation strategies beyond just reintroducing carnivores. The research was published by Mongabay India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. They were reintroduced a few years later and have since re-established populations. While carnivore reintroduction has been considered as an important strategy to restore ecological balance, a recent study highlights that prey abundance and habitat quality also play an essential role. The crisis may prompt Indian policymakers to explore domestic production or alternative inputs to mitigate supply risks.

articles.title: Kuwait hit by drone, missile fire

Iran and Kuwait exchanged fire, raising concerns about regional stability and the potential impact on global oil supplies.

articles.title: Crude oil prices rebound from six-week low amidst US-Iran deal uncertainties
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have risen after hitting a six-week low, amid ongoing uncertainties around a potential US-Iran nuclear deal. The situation involves 109 large oil vessels, some stranded near the Strait of Hormuz—a critical chokepoint for global oil trade—since regional conflicts began. Only 29 vessels have since passed through, highlighting supply chain risks. India, a major oil importer, could face price volatility and energy security concerns due to these developments.
articles.raw_summary: According to Bloomberg data, 29 out of the 109 big vessels, capable of hauling over 7,00,000 barrels or more, which were stranded at or near the Strait of Hormuz when the Iran war began and the route was effectively shut, have managed to cross the chokepoint.

articles.title: Gaganyaan Mission: Shubhanshu Shukla says human spaceflight is a big leap for India
articles.summary: A year after becoming the first Indian to visit the International Space Station, astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla is preparing for ISRO's Gaganyaan mission. Drawing on his experience as an Indian Air Force test pilot, Shukla says India's first human spaceflight programme will be a transformative milestone that strengthens the country's position in global space exploration.

articles.title: WATCH: Badrinath pilgrims stranded for hours as massive traffic jam hits NH-7 near Joshimath
articles.summary: Thousands of pilgrims traveling to Badrinath were stranded for hours due to a massive traffic jam on NH-7 near Joshimath. Authorities responded by activating an emergency token-based traffic system and deploying additional personnel to clear the congestion. The incident underscores challenges in managing high-traffic pilgrimage routes.
articles.raw_summary: A massive traffic jam on the Badrinath National Highway near Joshimath brought vehicular movement to a near standstill on Sunday, leaving thousands of Char Dham Yatra pilgrims stranded. Authorities activated an emergency token-based traffic management system and deployed additional personnel to clear the congestion.

articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

articles.title: Explained: NSE extends F&O trading by 10 minutes. What changes for traders?
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) will extend futures and options (F&O) trading by 10 minutes to 3:40 pm starting August 3, 2026, and introduce a Closing Auction Session (CAS) framework in the equity derivatives segment. The new auction-based mechanism aims to improve price discovery and align settlements between cash and derivatives markets. This change is driven by SEBI's regulatory framework to enhance market efficiency.
articles.raw_summary: The NSE will introduce the Closing Auction Session (CAS) framework in the equity derivatives segment from August 3, 2026. While market closing time will be extended by 10 minutes to 3:40 pm, the key change is the adoption of an auction-based mechanism to determine closing prices, aimed at improving price discovery and aligning cash and derivatives market settlements.

articles.title: Doctor accused of raping 15-year-old patient at Haryana hospital
articles.summary: A 15-year-old patient was allegedly raped by a doctor at a government hospital in Haryana. After she began bleeding, she was moved to the emergency ward where she disclosed the incident to senior doctors. The case raises concerns about child safety in healthcare settings and may prompt calls for stricter oversight in medical facilities. No policy action has been announced yet, but the incident could lead to demands for reforms in hospital governance and child protection laws.
articles.raw_summary: The girl was later moved to the emergency ward after she began bleeding. It was there that she confided in senior doctors about the incident.

articles.title: What's a Honey Singh song doing in the CBSE row? Teen hacker's mic-drop meme as board 'admits' gaps
articles.summary: An ethical hacker, Nisarga Adhikary, along with two other teenagers, has exposed flaws in the CBSE's On-Screen Marking (OSM) system for Class 12 exams. The controversy has drawn attention to potential gaps in the board's evaluation process. The CBSE has acknowledged some issues, raising concerns about the reliability of the system. The incident highlights the need for stronger safeguards in educational assessment policies.
articles.raw_summary: Ethical hacker Nisarga Adhikary is one of three teenagers whose posts have driven the controversy over CBSE's On-Screen Marking (OSM) system for Class 12.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why India wants to go back to standard edible oil pack sizes
articles.summary: The government plans to bring back standard pack sizes for edible oils on complaints that similar-looking pouches with different quantities and prices were making value-for-money comparisons difficult for buyers. The move aims to improve transparency and ease shopping choices.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Why Google was fined Rs 30 lakh in Hindware trademark case
articles.summary: Google must pay Hindware 30 lakh rupees. The Delhi High Court ruled Google infringed on Hindware's trademark. Google is permanently stopped from using 'HINDWARE' as an advertising keyword. This decision impacts digital advertising and trademark law in India. Competitors used Google's platform to show their ads when users searched for Hindware products.
